implementation.name: EventList
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Displays a list of events, provides UI for editing, deleting, and creating events. Calls backend endpoints via props or inline API calls.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render a list of events
For each event:
    Display event details
    Provide Edit and Delete buttons
Provide a button to create a new event
On Edit/Delete/Create, call corresponding handler functions

implementation.name: EventCard
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Displays individual event details, provides UI for editing and deleting a single event.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Receive event data as props
Display event name, description, start date, end date
Provide Edit and Delete buttons
On Edit/Delete, call handler functions passed via props

implementation.name: EventForm
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Provides a form for creating or editing an event, collects event name, description, start date, and end date.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Render input fields for event name, description, start date, end date
On submit:
    Validate input
    Call API to create or update event
    On success, notify parent component

implementation.name: create_event
implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: API endpoint for creating a new event. Receives event data from frontend, calls service to create event.
implementation.pseudo_code:
Receive POST request with event data
Call EventService.create_event with event data
Return created event response

implementation.name: TaskService.update_task

implementation.pseudo_code:
Given task_id and TaskUpdate data:
  - Fetch task by id
    - If not found, raise error
  - If event_id is provided:
    - Check if new event exists
      - If not, raise error
    - Update task.event_id
  - Update other fields
  - Save via TaskRepository.update
  - Return TaskResponse

implementation.name: TaskRepository.delete
implementation.pseudo_code:
Delete task from DB
Commit

missing_implementations: There is no explicit enforcement that each task is always associated with exactly one event after deletion. If a task is deleted, it is removed, but if an event is deleted, associated tasks may be deleted (as implied in EventList), but this is not explicitly shown in backend logic.
There is no explicit endpoint or function for unassigning a task from an event without deleting the task (i.e., making a task 'unassigned'). The current logic only allows reassignment or deletion.

implementation.name: TaskService.create_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF event with event_id exists

    CREATE Task object with title, description, status, event_id
    CALL TaskRepository.create with Task object
    RETURN TaskResponse for created task
ELSE
    RAISE HTTP 404 error

RETURN Task object

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Sends a PUT request to the backend to update the task details.

FUNCTION updateTask(taskId, data)
    SEND PUT request to /api/tasks/{taskId} with data
    RETURN response

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Receives the PUT request from frontend, invokes TaskService to update the task.
implementation.name: update_task (API endpoint)

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Contains business logic for updating task fields, validating event existence, and updating timestamps.
implementation.name: TaskService.update_task
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION update_task(task_id, task_data)
    FETCH task by id
    IF task not found
        RAISE 404 error
    FOR each field in task_data
        IF field is not None
            UPDATE task field
    IF event_id changed
        VALIDATE event exists
        UPDATE task.event_id
    UPDATE task.updated_at
    CALL repository.update(task)
    RETURN updated task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Commits the updated task object to the database and refreshes it.
implementation.name: TaskRepository.update
implementation.pseudo_code:
FUNCTION update(task)
    COMMIT changes to database
    REFRESH task object
    RETURN task

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: UI handler for deleting a task. Invoked when the user clicks the delete button on a TaskCard. Calls the deleteTask API and triggers UI update.
implementation.name: handleDelete
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF user confirms deletion
    TRY
        Call deleteTask(id) API
        Call onDelete() to refresh UI
    CATCH error
        Log error
        Show alert 'Failed to delete task'
